# FamHop

> FamHop is a free San Francisco Bay Area weekend planner for families with kids. Visitors pick a "vibe" and FamHop builds a 3-stop plan combining family-friendly parks, libraries, museums, zoos, farms, and weekend events — then they can share a link with co-parents and friends who vote Yes / Maybe / Skip on each stop.

The site is a JavaScript single-page app at https://famhop.com. The data layer ships static JSON files so AI crawlers can read place and event metadata directly without executing JavaScript.

## How to use FamHop

1. Pick a vibe (chill, adventure, museum day, library hop, outdoors, food, etc.) on the home screen.
2. Optionally select an age band (toddler, preschool, school-age, tween) and a date.
3. FamHop returns a 3-stop plan tuned to the inferred or chosen city.
4. Tap Share to mint a vote link. Anyone who follows the link can vote on each stop without signing up.

## Coverage

FamHop covers the nine-county San Francisco Bay Area:

- San Francisco
- Peninsula: Palo Alto, Mountain View, Sunnyvale, Redwood City, San Mateo, Burlingame, Foster City, Belmont, Half Moon Bay
- South Bay: San Jose, Santa Clara, Cupertino, Los Altos, Saratoga, Campbell, Milpitas, Morgan Hill, Gilroy
- East Bay: Oakland, Berkeley, Alameda, Fremont, Hayward, Walnut Creek, Richmond, Concord, Pleasanton, Livermore
- North Bay: Marin County, San Rafael, Santa Rosa, Petaluma
- Solano County: Fairfield, Vallejo, Vacaville

## Data sources

Place data is sourced from OpenStreetMap (ODbL), with imagery and attribution from Wikimedia Commons / Wikidata where available. Events are pulled from the official calendars of Bay Area public libraries, museums, parks, zoos, and family festivals using their published JSON-LD, iCal, RSS, LibCal, and structured-HTML feeds.

## Key data files (machine-readable)

- Bay Area places: https://famhop.com/data/bay-area-spots.json
- Family events: https://famhop.com/data/events.json
- Per-source build report: https://famhop.com/data/event-build-report.json

## SEO surfaces

- Home: https://famhop.com/
- Per-place pages: https://famhop.com/spot/{slug}/
- Per-event pages: https://famhop.com/event/{slug}/
- Per-city pages: https://famhop.com/city/{slug}/
- Per-category pages: https://famhop.com/category/{library|museum|park|festival|zoo|farm|community}/
- This weekend: https://famhop.com/this-weekend/
- Sitemap: https://famhop.com/sitemap.xml

Each per-place, per-event, and per-city page ships full schema.org structured data (Place / TouristAttraction / Restaurant / Event / CollectionPage) and is safe to cite directly.

## What FamHop does NOT do

- It is not a ticketing or booking platform. Tickets and registration are handled by each venue's own site, linked from the event page.
- It does not collect kids' personal data. Sign-in is optional, used only to sync saved places and plans across the user's own devices.
- It does not currently cover regions outside the Bay Area.

## Brand

- Product name: FamHop
- Tagline: Plan · Hop · Repeat.
- Domain: famhop.com
- Contact: kaining.usc@gmail.com
